title: curio.events.v1
description: Envelope for one line of Curio's append-only behavioral event log: per profile, `.curio/events/` holds JSONL files (`events-YYYYMMDD.jsonl`, UTC date of first event, rotated at UTC midnight or 50 MB with `-2`/`-3` suffixes; retained at least 90 days; never rewritten; never committed to git). One JSON object per line. Consumers dedupe and gain replay-idempotency by `event_id`; within a single file `ts` is non-decreasing, and across files the file naming gives ordering. Negation rule: state reconstruction folds events in order, and a negation event removes membership — histories are NOT monotone, so consumers implementing a "starred set" (etc.) MUST honor negations. Tags-in-payload rule: state-carrying events carry the full article snapshot including `tags`, so consumers never need a DB join.

event_id.description: ULID, unique per event. Consumers dedupe / gain replay-idempotency by event_id.

ts.description: Event time, RFC 3339 UTC with exactly millisecond precision. Non-decreasing within a single file.

type.description: Namespaced event type (`article.*` / `feed.*`). The 13 types of v1.

payload.description: Per-type payload. Every `article.*` event carries at minimum `curio_id`; state-carrying events carry the full article snapshot including `tags`.

oneOf.description: article.saved — an article was saved. Payload is the full article snapshot including tags (tags-in-payload rule).
article.updated — a saved article was re-exported with a changed checksum. Payload is the same full snapshot shape as article.saved.
article.starred — the article joined the starred set. State-carrying: payload includes tags. Negated by a later article.unstarred for the same curio_id.
article.unstarred — NEGATION of article.starred: folding events in order, this removes the article from the starred set. Histories are not monotone; consumers MUST honor this negation.
article.read_later.added — the article joined the read-later set. State-carrying: payload includes tags. Negated by a later article.read_later.removed for the same curio_id.
article.read_later.removed — NEGATION of article.read_later.added: folding events in order, this removes the article from the read-later set. Consumers MUST honor this negation.
article.archived — the article was archived. Negated by a later article.unarchived for the same curio_id.
article.unarchived — NEGATION of article.archived: folding events in order, this removes the article from the archived set. Consumers MUST honor this negation.
article.opened — the article was opened for reading; dwell_ms optionally records reading dwell time in milliseconds.
article.tagged — a single tag was added to the article. Negated, per tag, by a later article.untagged with the same curio_id and tag.
article.untagged — NEGATION of article.tagged for the same tag: folding events in order, this removes the tag from the article. Consumers MUST honor this negation.
feed.added — a feed subscription was added. Negated by a later feed.removed for the same feed URL.
feed.removed — NEGATION of feed.added: folding events in order, this removes the feed subscription. Consumers MUST honor this negation.

curioId.description: UUIDv7 in canonical lowercase hyphenated form. Article identity — assigned at save, never changes.

eventId.description: ULID: 26 characters of Crockford base32 (uppercase, no I/L/O/U), first character 0-7.

checksum.description: SHA-256 digest in lowercase hex with the literal `sha256:` prefix. Change token ONLY — never identity.

timestampMillis.description: RFC 3339 UTC timestamp with exactly millisecond precision, `Z`-suffixed.

timestampUtc.description: RFC 3339 UTC timestamp, `Z`-suffixed, fractional seconds optional.

articleSnapshotPayload.description: Full article snapshot carried by state-carrying events (article.saved / article.updated), including tags — the tags-in-payload rule means consumers never need a DB join.

feed.description: Feed URL the article came from; null if saved manually.

feed_title.description: Human-readable feed name; null if unknown or manual save.

tags.description: Curio-assigned tags at event time (tags-in-payload rule).

published.description: Publication time, RFC 3339 UTC; null if unknown.

destination.description: Named destination the note was exported to. Destination names, never raw filesystem paths.

path.description: Note path relative to the destination root.

curioIdWithTagsPayload.description: State-carrying membership payload: the article's identity plus its tags at event time (tags-in-payload rule).

curioIdOnlyPayload.description: Minimal article payload: identity only. Used by negation events (which carry no tags) and simple state flips.

openedPayload.description: article.opened payload: identity plus optional dwell time.

dwell_ms.description: Reading dwell time in milliseconds. Optional.

tagChangePayload.description: Single-tag change payload for article.tagged / article.untagged. article.untagged negates article.tagged for the same (curio_id, tag).

feedAddedPayload.description: feed.added payload: the subscribed feed plus its tags at event time.

feed_title.description: Human-readable feed name; null if unknown.

feedRemovedPayload.description: feed.removed payload: the unsubscribed feed URL. Negates feed.added for the same feed.
